revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[zh:飞路塔]]
{{infobox religious building
| Name = Feilu Tower
| image = 飞路塔 01.jpg
| Description = Feilu Tower and the inscription of the Cultural Heritage Site
| Grade = Provincial-Level Cultural Heritage Site
| Province = Fujian Province
| City/County = 
| Location = {{ill|Ningde|en|Ningde|qid=Q68919|lt=Ningde City|1=Ningde}}{{ill|Xiapu|en|Xiapu|qid=Q241877|lt=Xiapu County|1=Xiapu}}{{ill|Yantian She Ethnic Township|de|Yantian She Ethnic Township|ja|塩田畲族郷|zh|盐田畲族乡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yantian She Ethnic Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Beiyang Village, 2 km southeast
| Coordinates = {{Coord|26|53|27.49|N|119|54|51.23|E|display=inline,title}}
| Category = Ancient Architecture
| Era = Ming
| ID = 
| Registration = January 28, 2013
}}

'''Feilu Tower''' (飞路塔) is located in the {{ill|Fujian|en|Fujian|qid=Q41705|lt=Fujian Province|1=Fujian}} {{ill|Xiapu|en|Xiapu|qid=Q241877|lt=Xiapu County|1=Xiapu}} {{ill|Yantian She Ethnic Township|de|Yantian She Ethnic Township|ja|塩田畲族郷|zh|盐田畲族乡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yantian She Ethnic Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Beiyang Village. It is a {{ill|sculpture tower|de|sculpture tower|ja|造像塔|zh|造像塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sculpture tower|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} built in the early period of the {{ill|Ming Dynasty|de|Ming Dynasty|ja|明朝|zh|明朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ming Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明|1=Ming dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9903}} by believers of the {{ill|Mingjiao|de|Mingjiao|ja|明教|zh|明教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mingjiao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐代三夷教|1=Three Persian religions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11418516}}, and is one of the remaining Mingjiao structures in Xiapu County. The tower was destroyed during the {{ill|Cultural Revolution|en|Cultural Revolution|qid=Q8690|lt=Cultural Revolution|1=Cultural Revolution}}, but was rebuilt by local villagers in 2003. After being discovered during the third national cultural relics survey that began in 2007 in Xiapu County, Feilu Tower was subsequently included in the eighth batch of {{ill|Xiapu County Cultural Heritage Sites|de|Xiapu County Cultural Heritage Sites|ja|霞浦県文物保護単位|zh|霞浦县文物保护单位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiapu County Cultural Heritage Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 2010 and in the eighth batch of {{ill|Fujian Province Cultural Heritage Sites|de|Fujian Province Cultural Heritage Sites|ja|福建省文物保護単位|zh|福建省文物保护单位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujian Province Cultural Heritage Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 2013.<ref name=":0">{{cite web |author=Fujian Provincial People's Government |date=2013-01-28 |title=Notice from the Fujian Provincial People's Government on the Announcement of the Eighth Batch of Provincial-Level Cultural Heritage Sites and Their Protection Scope (Minzheng [2013] No. 9) |url=https://zfgb.fujian.gov.cn/3390 |access-date=2022-08-12 |archive-date=2022-08-11 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220811183127/https://zfgb.fujian.gov.cn/3390 |dead-url=no }} {{PD-notice}}</ref>

== History ==
According to the inscription on the tower, Feilu Tower was built in the sixth year of the {{ill|Hongwu Emperor|de|Hongwu Emperor|ja|洪武|zh|洪武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongwu Emperor|1=Hongwu era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1131602}} period of the Ming Dynasty (1374) by local believers of the Mingjiao. At that time, although the Ming founder Zhu Yuanzhang had issued orders prohibiting Mingjiao, the inscriptions on the side pillars indicate that public fundraising took place, showing that Mingjiao still had a certain mass support in the area.<ref name=":1">{{Cite journal |author=Yang Fuxue |author2=Li Xiaoyan |author3=Peng Xiaojing |title=Major Findings from the Survey of Manichaean Relics in Fujian |url=http://www.silkroads.org.cn/portal.php?mod=view&aid=12663 |journal=Studies in Religious Research |language=en |publication-place=Chengdu |publication-date=2017 |issue=04 |page=259-271 |issn=1006-1312 |access-date=2024-07-18 |archive-date=2024-07-18 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240718174658/http://www.silkroads.org.cn/portal.php?mod=view&aid=12663 |dead-url=no }}</ref> After its completion, over nearly 600 years, Feilu Tower experienced the {{ill|Qing Dynasty|de|Qing Dynasty|ja|清朝|zh|清朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Qing Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清|1=Qing dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8733}}, the period of the {{ill|Republic of China (Mainland Period)|de|Republic of China (Mainland Period)|ja|中華民國大陸時期|zh|中华民国|lt=Republic of China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Republic of China (Mainland Period)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the establishment of the {{ill|China|en|China|qid=Q148|lt=People's Republic of China|1=China}}, until during the {{ill|Cultural Revolution|en|Cultural Revolution|qid=Q8690|lt=Cultural Revolution|1=Cultural Revolution}} the tower was dismantled, with its components scattered and abandoned in several locations.<ref name=":8" />

In 2003, local villagers voluntarily recovered the tower components from the mountains and rebuilt Feilu Tower at its original site, adding a {{ill|concrete|de|concrete|ja|混凝土|zh|混凝土|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/concrete|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コンクリート|1=Concrete|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22657}} building and surrounding walls to protect the tower.<ref name=":8">{{Cite book|title=Ancient Architecture of Ningde|last=Zhou Qi et al. |publisher=Fujian People's Publishing House|year=2014|isbn=978-7-211-07042-8|location=Fuzhou|pages=94-96}}</ref> Between 2007 and 2011, during the third national cultural relics survey, Feilu Tower was discovered and identified as a relic of Mingjiao. Together with the Three Buddha Tower in Xiapu County, it was recognized as a historically significant structure for the study of Manichaeism.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Ancient Architecture of Fujian and Taiwan|last=Chen Mingshi|publisher=Fujian Art Publishing House|year=2018|isbn=978-7-5393-3888-0|location=Fuzhou|pages=361}}</ref> In 2010, Feilu Tower was included in the eighth batch of Xiapu County Cultural Heritage Sites,<ref name=":11" /> and in 2013, the {{ill|Fujian Provincial People's Government|de|Fujian Provincial People's Government|ja|福建省人民政府|zh|福建省人民政府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujian Provincial People's Government|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} declared it a provincial-level cultural heritage site in the eighth batch.<ref name=":0" />

== Architecture ==
Feilu Tower is situated on the roadside at the junction of the Gangtou Mountain Range and Yachi Mountain, facing south with the {{ill|Yantian Township Government|de|Yantian Township Government|ja|盐田乡政府|zh|盐田乡政府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yantian Township Government|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} visible to the west and the county town of Xiapu in the east. The tower is embedded within the newly constructed {{ill|Sizhou Buddha Pavilion|de|Sizhou Buddha Pavilion|ja|泗洲佛祖|zh|泗洲佛|lt=Sizhou Buddha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sizhou Buddha Pavilion|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and is a single-story, quadrangular, stone sculpture tower made of {{ill|granite|de|granite|ja|花岗岩|zh|花岗岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/granite|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It covers an area of 35.3 square meters, is single-storied, and has a total height of 3 meters, with a base height of 0.95 meters and width of 0.90 meters, and a tower body height of 0.90 meters and width of 1.22 meters. The niche on the tower bears the inscription "Feilu Tower Four Continents Buddha", and the left and right of the niche door feature couplets in incised regular script, very similar to those found in the {{ill|Grass Hut|de|Grass Hut|ja|草庵|zh|草庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grass Hut|1=sōan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24897528}} of {{ill|Jin River (Fujian)|en|Jin River (Fujian)|qid=Q1031649|lt=Jinjiang|1=Jin River (Fujian)}}. The inner side is inscribed with the couplet "Pure Light, Great Wisdom", while the outer side pillars bear the inscription "Established on a Propitious Day in the Year of Jia-Yin in the Hongwu Period by the Benefactor of Dongfeng and the Autumn Association of Zhongxuan, raised by locals", with character widths varying around 0.1 meters. Inside the niche are three shallow relief statues; the central main Buddha statue, measuring 0.6 meters in height, is the {{ill|Mani Light Buddha|de|Mani Light Buddha|ja|摩尼光佛|zh|摩尼光佛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mani Light Buddha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, whose head was destroyed and later repaired with cement; the two smaller Buddha statues on either side are 0.4 meters tall; in front of the niche stands an incense burner named "Heaven and Earth Furnace". On the left side of the tower, there is a figure of the {{ill|Tudigong|en|Tudigong|qid=Q2670522|lt=Tudigong|1=Tudigong}}, while on the right is a divination booth. The divination inscriptions exhibit a slight Manichaean character and bear some resemblance to the divination poems inside the Grass Hut. On the side of the tower base, there is a piece of {{ill|diorite|de|diorite|ja|輝緑岩|zh|辉绿岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diorite|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ドレライト|1=dolerite|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1235463}} stone component measuring 0.58 meters in length, 0.3 meters in width, and 0.2 meters in height, and at the corner of the wall stands a plaque offered by believers from Beiyang Township (now Beiyang Village), with several banners erected by worshippers outside the building.<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" /><ref name=":11">{{Cite book|title=Cultural Heritage of Xiapu (Volume 27 of Xiapu Historical Materials)|last=Xiapu County Committee of the CPPCC, Fujian Province|publisher=Ningde Municipal Bureau of Culture and Publishing|year=2010|location=Xiapu County}}</ref>
